# VERIFY: #65 — Build roadmap play for Meridian OS (IDSD framework)
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output — updated with acceptance scenarios -->

**Intent:** Verify that the plan-roadmap high-order play implementation satisfies the spec — produces correct artifacts, respects all constraints, properly delegates, maintains audience separation, enforces progressive enrichment, and gates correctly.

---

## Acceptance Scenarios

### S1 — SCOPE phase produces scoped epics from locked vision

**Given** a locked vision.md exists at the provided path
**When** `/plan-roadmap --phase scope --vision <path>` is invoked
**Then** product-strategist is called exactly once (scope-roadmap-epics), tech-designer is called exactly once (feasibility), a checkpoint is written at `.meridian/project/product/checkpoint/plan-roadmap/{ts}.md` with `current_phase: scope`, and evidence is committed via repo-orchestrator

---

### S2 — DRAFT phase generates brief before any agentic artifacts

**Given** a SCOPE checkpoint exists with scoped_epics and feasibility_flags
**When** `/plan-roadmap --phase draft --vision <path>` is invoked
**Then** a roadmap brief is generated and presented to the user BEFORE roadmap.md, roadmap-review.md, or roadmap-engineering.md are written — those files do not exist until after Tether

---

### S3 — Brief satisfies the 30-minute constraint (C-BRIEF-1)

**Given** the draft-roadmap-brief skill has run
**When** the output brief is inspected
**Then** it contains exactly six sections (Bet, Story, Decision Table, What We're Not Doing, The Asks, Key Assumptions), the decision table has ≤8 rows, and `c_brief_1_pass: true` is in the skill output contract

---

### S4 — Brief contains no decision-irrelevant technical content (C-BRIEF-2)

**Given** the draft-roadmap-brief skill has run
**When** the brief is inspected for technical content
**Then** no NFR targets, implementation details, work packages, or specific technical choices appear — only hard dependencies, foundation investments, or migration costs that justify sequencing decisions — and `c_brief_2_pass: true` is in the skill output contract

---

### S5 — Feedback loop adjusts and re-presents brief

**Given** the brief has been presented and the user responds with plain-text feedback (not Tether or Vanish)
**When** the feedback is parsed (e.g. "move LTM to P1")
**Then** product-strategist is re-invoked to adjust the brief, the updated brief is re-presented, and the feedback_cycles counter in the checkpoint increments by 1

---

### S6 — Feedback loop enforces maximum 3 cycles

**Given** the feedback loop has cycled 3 times without a Tether
**When** the user provides a 4th round of feedback
**Then** the play presents the current brief with Tether/Vanish only — no further adjustment cycles are offered

---

### S7 — Full agentic artifacts generated only after Tether

**Given** the brief has been presented
**When** the user types Tether
**Then** product-strategist is invoked with compound intent (draft-roadmap + generate-business-review + generate-engineering-view), roadmap.md is written at `.meridian/project/product/{slug}/roadmap.md`, PM view is written, engineering view is written, and the checkpoint brief_status is updated to `approved`

---

### S8 — Each epic in roadmap.md has IDD core fully filled

**Given** roadmap.md has been generated post-Tether
**When** any epic section is inspected
**Then** it contains Intent (2–3 paragraphs), Constraints (in scope / out of scope / must not break), Acceptance Scenarios (given/when/then form), and Failure Conditions — and none of these sections are empty or contain placeholder text

---

### S9 — Acceptance scenarios are binary testable

**Given** an epic's Acceptance Scenarios section in roadmap.md
**When** each scenario is read
**Then** every scenario follows given/when/then structure and its outcome is either definitively pass or fail — no scenario uses vague language like "works correctly" or "performs well"

---

### S10 — Technical context and blast radius are empty placeholders at roadmap time

**Given** roadmap.md has been generated by draft-roadmap
**When** the Technical Context and Blast Radius sections of any epic are inspected
**Then** both sections contain `<!-- status: empty -->` markers, values are TBD, and no systems, packages, or architectural directions have been filled in

---

### S11 — epics_completeness tracks fill state per section

**Given** draft-roadmap has run and produced roadmap.md
**When** the skill output contract is inspected
**Then** `epics_completeness` contains one entry per epic, each with fields: `intent`, `constraints`, `scenarios`, `failures` all set to `filled`, and `technical`, `blast_radius` both set to `empty`

---

### S12 — Product view contains zero engineering content

**Given** the PM view has been generated at `.meridian/project/product/{slug}/reviews/roadmap-review.md`
**When** the file is inspected
**Then** it contains no work packages, component names, file paths, complexity ratings, technical risks, or implementation details of any kind

---

### S13 — Engineering view contains zero business content

**Given** the engineering view has been generated at `.meridian/project/product/{slug}/roadmap-engineering.md`
**When** the file is inspected
**Then** it contains no business value statements, revenue projections, market context, user personas, or customer problem descriptions

---

### S14 — Resume from pending brief state

**Given** a DRAFT phase checkpoint exists with `brief_status: pending` and a valid `brief_path`
**When** `/plan-roadmap --resume` is invoked with no other arguments
**Then** the brief is loaded from `brief_path`, re-presented to the user with feedback instructions, and the play continues the feedback loop from the current `feedback_cycles` count — no vision path argument is required

---

### S15 — Resume from approved-but-ungenerated state

**Given** a DRAFT phase checkpoint exists with `brief_status: approved` and roadmap.md does not exist at the expected path
**When** `/plan-roadmap --resume` is invoked
**Then** the play jumps directly to artifact generation (DRAFT Step 4) without re-presenting the brief — product-strategist is invoked with compound intent to generate all three artifacts

---

### S16 — Pre-flight hard halt when vision is not LOCKED

**Given** a vision.md exists at the provided path with `status: DRAFT`
**When** `/plan-roadmap --phase draft --vision <path>` is invoked
**Then** the play halts immediately with C3's halt_message ("Vision is not LOCKED — run /discover-product --phase lock first") and no agent is invoked

---

### S17 — Pre-flight hard halt when vision path does not exist

**Given** no file exists at the provided vision path
**When** `/plan-roadmap --phase scope --vision <nonexistent-path>` is invoked
**Then** the play halts immediately with C1's halt_message and no agent is invoked

---

### S18 — Agent call budget is not exceeded

**Given** a complete SCOPE + DRAFT run has been executed
**When** the number of Task tool invocations across both phases is counted
**Then** the total is ≤5 and ideally exactly 3 (product-strategist scope, tech-designer feasibility, product-strategist compound draft)

---

### S19 — Vanish halts without generating artifacts

**Given** the brief has been presented to the user
**When** the user types Vanish
**Then** no roadmap.md, PM view, or engineering view is written, the checkpoint brief_status is set to `rejected`, evidence is written recording the halt, and evidence is committed via repo-orchestrator

---

### S20 — LOCK phase flips status and does not invoke agents

**Given** a roadmap.md exists with `status: DRAFT`
**When** `/plan-roadmap --phase lock` is invoked
**Then** the orchestrator updates roadmap.md `status` from `DRAFT` to `LOCKED` directly (no agent invoked), evidence is written, and the final report lists the artifact paths and Next Steps (`/manage-backlog`, `/plan-architecture`)

---

### S21 — Re-draft refused when roadmap is LOCKED

**Given** a roadmap.md exists with `status: LOCKED`
**When** `/plan-roadmap --phase draft --vision <path>` is invoked
**Then** the draft-roadmap skill returns a structured failure refusing to overwrite the LOCKED artifact, and the play halts with a message indicating the roadmap is locked

---

### S22 — All new components deploy correctly via sync-claude

**Given** all T1–T6 files have been created in `core/components/`
**When** `/sync-claude` is run
**Then** `~/.claude/skills/plan-roadmap/SKILL.md` exists, `~/.claude/skills/scope-roadmap-epics/SKILL.md` exists, `~/.claude/skills/draft-roadmap-brief/SKILL.md` exists, `~/.claude/skills/draft-roadmap/SKILL.md` exists, `~/.claude/skills/generate-engineering-view/SKILL.md` exists, and `~/.claude/agents/product-strategist.md` contains all 4 new skill entries

---

### S23 — Evidence committed on feature branch, not main

**Given** the play is running on a feature branch
**When** SCOPE or DRAFT phase completes and evidence is committed
**Then** the chore(stm) commit lands on the feature branch — not on main — and the working tree is clean after the commit

---

## Failure Conditions Reference

| Scenario | Failure Signal |
|----------|---------------|
| Vision not LOCKED at DRAFT/LOCK invocation | C3 hard halt |
| Vision path does not exist | C1 hard halt |
| Phase argument not scope/draft/lock | C2 hard halt |
| Brief violates C-BRIEF-1 | Skill self-check returns `c_brief_1_pass: false` — play re-invokes with violation details |
| Brief contains decision-irrelevant technical content | Skill self-check returns `c_brief_2_pass: false` — play re-invokes with violation details |
| Fewer than 3 epics scoped | product-strategist returns structured failure — play halts |
| User Vanishes at brief | No artifacts generated, checkpoint status: rejected |
| Roadmap LOCKED — re-draft attempted | draft-roadmap structured failure — play halts |
| Agent call budget exceeded | Guardian halts with F-budget failure |
